name: gudhi_wasserstein_significance
description: Statistical significance of the L0-L7 cascade persistence diagram vs N=100 random null cascades (random kill-layer assignments). Uses Wasserstein (hera) and bottleneck distances.

z3.reason: no SMT constraints in statistical test

gudhi.reason: primary: Rips persistence, hera Wasserstein, bottleneck distance

positive.test1_detail: Wasserstein distance of any diagram to itself must be 0.
positive.test2_detail: p-value = 0.0200. 2/100 null cascades produce persistence diagrams identical (W-dist < eps) to the real cascade. p < 0.05 means the real kill-layer structure is rare among random assignments.
positive.test4_detail: Real cascade has 3 H0 pairs. 100.0% of nulls match.
positive.test5_detail: Bottleneck: mean=1.9550, std=0.8127. Positive mean confirms most nulls differ topologically from real.

summary.interpretation: The real cascade's persistence diagram is compared to diagrams from random kill-layer assignments. A low p-value means the specific L4+L6 kill structure produces rare topological features.

negative.neg1_detail: Random cascade (kill at L0, L5): self-distance = 0.00e+00. Any diagram's Wasserstein distance to itself must be 0.
negative.neg2_detail: Cascade(L0,L5) vs Cascade(L7,L0): W=4.0000, bottleneck=2.0000. Distance is non-negative (metric axiom guaranteed).
negative.neg2_structure_detail: Different kill-layer pairs should usually produce different persistence diagrams.

boundary.boundary1_detail: All 23 killed at L4: 2 H0 pairs (expected 2). W-dist from real = 3.0000, bottleneck = 2.0000.
boundary.boundary2_detail: No-kill cascade: 1 H0 pair(s) (expected 1). W-dist from real = 2.0000, bottleneck = 1.0000.
boundary.boundary3_detail: Adjacent kill layers (L4,L5) vs real (L4,L6): W=2.0000, bottleneck=1.0000. Shows sensitivity to kill-layer spacing.
